title: Knautia fleischmannii
taxonomy: Knautia fleischmannii is a species of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae. It belongs to the genus Knautia and is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Europe. The plant is commonly known as the fleischmann's knapweed or fleischmann's knautia. As a member of the Caprifoliaceae family, Knautia fleischmannii is closely related to other plants like Lonicera and Viburnum. The plant's classification is further divided into two subtaxa, Knautia fleischmannii subsp. fleischmannii and Knautia fleischmannii subsp. montana.

intro: Knautia fleischmannii is a flowering plant species, also known as Fleischmann's knapweed, which is native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Knautia fleischmannii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
